purpose: Define the first Galaxy Guardians reference conformance metric set by scoring the playable preview against the current Galaxian source profile, promoted semantic event log, game-owned runtime gates, and Platinum boundary rules.

sourceEvidence.notes: This is a first 0.1 metric model. It is intentionally conservative because most Galaxian evidence is still promoted from reviewed contact sheets, waveform windows, broad sprite crop targets, and mixed-source cue candidates rather than final frame-level/acoustic extraction.

summary.releaseRead: Useful development preview evidence, but local gameplay review showed that sound, timing, pacing, and sprite fidelity are still not convincing enough for a beta-facing quick peek. The score table, wave progression, visible attract/score-table surfaces, frame-motion proxy, object-track proxy, runtime-vs-reference movement comparison, stage-rank pressure contract, sprite-reference extraction, broad sprite-grid targets, waveform/spectrogram audio comparison, stricter audio cue targets, mixed-source isolated cue candidates, and playtest-weighted conformance review now exist as dev-preview contracts; the highest-leverage next work is manually reviewed cue labeling/export, tighter component sprite crops, and a browser-reviewed motion-feel pass.

metricScale.0: No implemented or measured relationship to the reference.
metricScale.5: Coarse semantic relationship exists, but evidence is mostly manual/internal.
metricScale.8: Runtime behavior is gated and traceable to committed reference evidence.
metricScale.10: Frame-level or acoustic reference measurement is automated and release-ready.

categories.label: Reference source coverage
categories.currentRead: Three Galaxian sources are committed with manifests, contact sheets, waveform windows, spectrograms, frame indexes, frame-motion proxies, a CPU-only connected-component/object-track proxy pass, runtime-vs-reference movement comparison, sprite crop extraction, broad sprite-grid target extraction, audio reference comparison, and mixed-source cue-candidate analysis.
categories.remainingGap: Promote broad object/crop/cue proxies into final sprite recognition and manually labeled isolated acoustic cue matching.

categories.label: Promoted semantic event coverage
categories.currentRead: The preview covers the core runtime promoted events: formation entry, rack complete, dives, flagship/escort pressure, single-shot fire, shot resolution, wrap/return, plus an owned score table, stage-advance progression contract, and visible wait-mode/preview-modal attract surfaces.
categories.remainingGap: Score-table and attract-screen evidence still needs frame-level extraction and pixel-layout comparison against the reference footage.

categories.label: Formation and rack timing
categories.currentRead: Formation entry, settle, rack complete, and first-dive delay are modeled and gated. The latest runtime pass shortened entry and first-dive timing after playtest review found the preview too slow.
categories.remainingGap: The object proxy is still connected-component tracking rather than final sprite recognition, so runtime timing needs browser-reviewed feel scoring beside instrumented reference tracks.

categories.label: Movement and pressure model
categories.currentRead: Scout dives, flagship dives, escort joins, enemy shots, and wrap/return pressure are implemented and gated. The latest pass increases dive speed, dive acceleration, enemy-shot pressure, wrap cadence, and bounded stage-rank pressure so stages three and five are measurably more urgent than stage one.
categories.remainingGap: The preview still feels proxy-tuned rather than Galaxian-tight; browser review and sprite-recognized dive-path targets are needed before beta-facing release.

categories.label: Single-shot threat and scoring
categories.currentRead: Single-shot firing, enemy shots, role-specific score values, player loss, game-over behavior, owned score table, wave clear, and stage advance are implemented without Aurora capture/dual-fighter semantics.
categories.remainingGap: The score table remains a dev-preview contract rather than a frame-extracted Galaxian table, and persistent public scoring is still intentionally blocked.

categories.label: Visual alien identity
categories.currentRead: Flagship, escort, scout, and player interceptor visuals are smaller, separate from Aurora, and gated for role readability. The latest pass separates escort and scout silhouettes, measures 27 rack/diver/player crops, and derives four broad reference-crop grid targets with an average silhouette similarity of 0.622.
categories.remainingGap: Sprites are still hand-authored preview approximations; the broad crop grids need tighter manually reviewed component crops before the graphics can feel close to Galaxian.

categories.label: Audio character and reference fit
categories.currentRead: Runtime cue IDs and role-separated cue shapes are gated, the latest pass adds filtered-noise layers to the player shot, enemy shot, dive, hit, and loss cues, the audio comparison analyzes 9 Galaxian waveform/spectrogram windows with PCM proxy metrics, and the new cue-candidate analysis finds 64 short/high-energy source candidates across shot, hit, dive, and loss-like families.
categories.remainingGap: This is still the weakest Guardians area: the detected candidates are mixed-source and need manual labeling/export before they can replace synthetic cue targets or support beta-facing audio claims.

categories.label: Platform and game boundaries
categories.currentRead: The preview remains dev-only, does not inherit Aurora capture/dual/challenge/scoring mechanics, and uses Platinum only through shared capability boundaries.
categories.remainingGap: Keep this mandatory as Guardians gets more real gameplay.

categories.label: Evidence durability
categories.currentRead: The reference profile, event log, identity artifacts, score/progression artifact, attract/score surface artifact, frame-motion artifact, object-track artifact, runtime-reference movement artifact, stage-rank pressure artifact, sprite extraction artifact, sprite-grid target artifact, audio comparison artifact, cue-candidate artifact, and 0.1 gates are committed and rerunnable.
categories.remainingGap: Add final component sprite/cue extraction and browser-reviewed beta-candidate evidence.

nextMetricPromotions: tighter manually reviewed sprite recognition from representative Galaxian crop components
Guardians attract and score-table pixel-layout comparison
manual labeling/export of isolated cue candidates into measured cue targets
browser-reviewed beta-candidate pass for the tuned movement slice
